_comment: v7 hotspots — optimized for BOTH ipSAE maximization AND FGF1 displacement. Reduced from v6 (6 residues) to v7 (4 residues) for tighter, more confidently-predicted interface.
campaign: FGFR2 selective binder, frontier-only ipSAE pipeline
selection_criteria: All four residues on rigid β-strands of D3 (low pAE → high ipSAE)
Compact 14 Å cluster (interface fits AlphaFold's TM-score-favored regime)
Cover top affinity (mCSM rank 1, 3, 5) for direct FGF1 displacement
Two CRAC residues (Y281, V317) for paralog selectivity via structural asymmetry

hotspots.rationale: Largest single-residue energetic contributor to FGF1 binding. Carboxylate forms salt-bridge with FGF1 K12 backbone (PISA-validated). Paralog-conserved (FGFR1 D282 also engages, BSA 24.9). On βC β-strand of D3 — AF2 predicts this region with pLDDT > 90, low pAE → high ipSAE.

BEST RESIDUE in the entire interface. Same β-strand as D283 (7.2 Å apart) — keeps the interface compact for ipSAE. mCSM top-5 affinity AND CRAC asymmetry: FGFR2 buries 49 A^2 of FGF1 surface, FGFR1's Y280 buries only 3 A^2. Same residue identity in both paralogs but different docking geometry — binder inherits the asymmetry. Dual purpose in one slot.

mCSM rank 3 affinity contributor. Amide group H-bonds FGF1 backbone (PISA). On βF/βG β-strand of D3 — rigid secondary structure. 8.6 Å from D283 → contributes to compact interface. Adjacent residues N318, D321 will be incidentally engaged (extends D3 coverage).

STRONGEST single-residue selectivity signal in the entire interface. PISA: V317 buries 127 A^2 of FGF1 in FGFR2; FGFR1's V316 buries ZERO. FGFR1 has nothing at this position to confuse a binder. Modest mCSM (-0.41) is the point — V317 is selectivity not affinity. On βF β-strand → rigid, AF2 places it confidently → high ipSAE.

dropped_from_v6.reason: On βC' loop (D2-D3 region) — loop residues have high pAE in AlphaFold predictions, dilute ipSAE. Despite mCSM rank 2 (-1.56) and biggest BSA (130 Å²), keeping it as an explicit hotspot hurts ranking. BindCraft hallucination will engage R251 incidentally because it's adjacent to D283 (7.3 Å), so we don't actually lose its contribution — we just stop biasing the model toward making contact at a pAE-noisy position.

On βC'-βD loop of D2 — loop region, high pAE, hurts ipSAE. Chemistry-flip selectivity (vs FGFR1 K172) is a nice-to-have but Y281 (CRAC dual) and V317 (CRAC king) already provide selectivity through structural asymmetry, which is more robust than chemistry-flip. Drop without losing selectivity.

rejected_in_earlier_rounds.reason: Paired-PISA refuted mCSM signal. FGFR1 K164 has BSA 7.5 vs FGFR2 R165 at 4.5 — FGFR1 actually engages MORE. Targeting R165 would not differentiate paralogs.
Actively harmful. FGFR1 P283 has BSA 36.3 vs FGFR2 A284 at 21.8. A binder targeting A284 would prefer FGFR1. Eliminate.
Not in either paralog's interface (BSA = 0 in both 1DJS and 1EVT). Geometric ghost. Was 6.7 Å from FGF1 — rim, not contact.

evidence_sources: PISA (per-residue BSA on 1DJS chain A vs 1EVT chain C, paired comparison)
mCSM-PPI2 (empirical alanine scan ΔΔG, trained on SKEMPI mutation database)
Multi-paralog MSA (FGFR1/2/3/4 from UniProt, BLOSUM62 + star alignment)
PeSTo (independent ML binding-site prediction on FGFR2 alone)
Plotnikov 2000 Cell — original 1DJS paper, validates affinity-vs-selectivity decoupling
Cao 2022 Nature — only published de novo FGFR binder; targets D3 backside, NOT FGF1 site (different problem)

ipsae_optimization_decisions.why_4_not_6: ipSAE is a TM-score-like normalized sum over interface residue pairs. Adding hotspots adds residue pairs to the denominator faster than it adds confident pairs to the numerator. 4 high-quality, rigid-anchor hotspots produce higher ipSAE than 6 hotspots that include loop residues. IPD's RFD3 docs cite 3-6 as the empirical sweet spot.
ipsae_optimization_decisions.why_only_d3_strands: The four chosen hotspots are ALL on β-strands of D3. AF2 predicts β-strand backbone with pLDDT > 90 typically, and PAE within strand pairs is ~2-3 Å. Loop residues like R251 (βC' loop, D247 area) have pLDDT ~70-80 and PAE ~5-8 Å — they dilute ipSAE.
ipsae_optimization_decisions.compactness: All four hotspots cluster in 14 Å diameter (D283-Y281=7.2, D283-N346=8.6, D283-V317=17.7). AlphaFold's d0 in TM-score normalization is length-dependent; for 50-residue binder d0 ≈ 4.6 Å. Compact interfaces fit this normalization regime; spread interfaces don't.
ipsae_optimization_decisions.why_HardTarget_in_BindCraft: HardTarget prediction protocol uses initial-guess complex prediction. Per IPD this increases complex confidence ~10-15% over default. Single biggest BindCraft lever for ipSAE.

expected_outcome.predicted_top_ipsae: 0.75-0.85 on the best designs (vs 0.55-0.65 with v6 6-hotspot list)
expected_outcome.predicted_selectivity_gap: Δ-ipSAE > 0.20 on the best designs (V317 + Y281 CRAC drives this)
expected_outcome.predicted_boltz2_affinity: -7 to -10 kcal/mol (sub-nM K_d range) on the best designs
